sourceMovie.title: Fedora
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Fedora for the same reason it broke through in 1978: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Billy Wilder keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mulholland Drive
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fedora clicked for you, Mulholland Drive (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Fedora?
faq.answer: Try Mulholland Drive, Remember, Wicked Little Letters—each shares DNA with Fedora while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Fedora?
faq.answer: Fedora (1978) is best described as Drama, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Fedora worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
